evals.prompt: Review an OSS-WS repository that contains CHANGELOG.md but no changelog.md. What should change?
evals.expected_output: The agent requires root changelog.md as the canonical OSS-WS changelog file and rejects CHANGELOG.md as canonical.
expectations: Requires changelog.md at the repository root
Requires the first heading to be # Changelog
Does not accept CHANGELOG.md as the canonical OSS-WS filename

evals.prompt: Rewrite these changelog bullets: 'feat: add templates', 'fixed typo', 'PR #12'.
evals.expected_output: Human-readable imperative changelog bullets that are self-contained and avoid raw commit or pull request title style.
expectations: Starts each changelog item with an imperative present-tense verb
Makes each changelog item understandable without the group heading
Avoids copying commit prefixes into changelog bullets
Does not copy pull request titles without curation

evals.prompt: A phase added skill.md, rules, evals, and OpenAI agent metadata. Verify whether the phase is complete.
evals.expected_output: The agent checks that root changelog.md was updated, intended files are staged together, and the phase can be committed as one coherent unit.
expectations: Requires changelog.md to reflect the phase
Checks related skill, rule, eval, and agent files for consistency
Treats commit creation as part of completing the phase when available

evals.prompt: Review this commit subject for OSS-WS compliance: 'Added commit docs and fixed random typo'.
evals.expected_output: The agent rejects the subject because it does not match type(scope): subject, is not lowercase, has no scope, and mixes unrelated work.
expectations: Requires type(scope): subject
Requires an allowed type
Requires a lowercase scope
Requires a lowercase subject
Flags unrelated work mixed into one commit

evals.prompt: Draft a commit message for adding a commit standard, commit guide, commit rules, commit evals, and a changelog update.
evals.expected_output: A coherent commit message using feat(phase): add draft commit standard.
expectations: Uses feat(phase): add draft commit standard
Treats specs, docs, rules, evals, and changelog as one coherent standard phase
Uses lowercase type, scope, and subject
Does not use imperative title case

evals.prompt: A commit changes a public template schema in a breaking way. Review the commit message.
evals.expected_output: The agent requires a scoped lowercase subject with ! after the scope, such as feat(template)!: require schema version, and expects migration context when needed.
expectations: Uses ! after the scope for breaking changes
Keeps the type, scope, and subject lowercase
Explains required user action in the body when needed

evals.prompt: Review a skill rule file that only says to see the guide or spec instead of stating the rule.
evals.expected_output: The agent rejects the rule file because OSS-WS skill rules must be self-contained and directly actionable.
expectations: Rejects pointer-only skill rules
Requires the actual behavior to be written in the rule file
Does not rely on path references as the primary instruction

evals.prompt: Review a Rust CLI that validates OSS-WS commit subjects, inspects git state, compares diffs, and groups changed files.
evals.expected_output: The agent expects a clap-based cross-platform CLI with git inspect, diff, analyze, commit validation, changelog check, and phase check commands.
expectations: Uses Rust and clap
Calls git with explicit command arguments
Supports git inspection
Supports staged and unstaged diff comparison
Groups changed files by OSS-WS area
Validates lowercase scoped commit subjects
Checks changelog and phase readiness
